all.en: In January, I signed up for my very first half-marathon, scheduled in April.
all.zh: 一月時，我報名了我的第一場半程馬拉松，比賽訂在四月。

all.en: Twenty-one kilometers sounded scary, since I had never run more than five before.
all.zh: 二十一公里聽起來很可怕，因為我之前從來沒跑過超過五公里。

all.en: A running coach helped me design a twelve-week training plan with mixed workouts.
all.zh: 一位跑步教練幫我安排了一份為期十二週、訓練內容多元的計畫。

all.en: On Mondays and Wednesdays, I did short and fast runs around the city park.
all.zh: 星期一和星期三，我在城市公園做短距離的快跑。

all.en: On Saturdays, I went for long, slow runs to build my endurance gradually.
all.zh: 星期六，我則進行長距離的慢跑，慢慢累積耐力。

all.en: At first, my knees ached and my breathing was uneven on hills.
all.zh: 剛開始，我膝蓋會痛，上坡時呼吸也很不順。

all.en: The coach reminded me to invest in proper running shoes to protect my joints.
all.zh: 教練提醒我，要投資一雙合適的跑鞋來保護關節。

all.en: He also taught me how to stretch and roll my muscles after every run.
all.zh: 他也教我每次跑完後如何伸展和按摩肌肉。

all.en: By week eight, I could finish fifteen kilometers without stopping for water.
all.zh: 到了第八週，我已經可以連跑十五公里不停下來喝水。

all.en: On race day, I felt nervous but well prepared as I stood at the starting line.
all.zh: 比賽當天，我站在起跑線上，雖然緊張，但已準備充分。

all.en: The crowd cheered for every runner, which gave me energy when I felt tired.
all.zh: 群眾為每位跑者加油，在我感到疲倦時給了我力量。

all.en: Crossing the finish line in just under two hours was the proudest moment of my year.
all.zh: 在不到兩小時內衝過終點線，是我這一年最自豪的時刻。

all.en: After the race, I rested for one full week before considering my next goal.
all.zh: 比賽結束後，我先完整休息了一週，才開始思考下一個目標。

all.en: My friends suggested I sign up for a full marathon scheduled in October.
all.zh: 朋友們建議我報名十月舉行的全程馬拉松。

all.en: I was tempted, but my coach warned me about jumping into longer distances too fast.
all.zh: 我有點心動，但教練警告我不要太快挑戰更長的距離。

all.en: Instead, he advised me to focus on speed work and core strength for a few months.
all.zh: 他改建議我先把幾個月的重心放在速度訓練和核心肌力上。

all.en: I joined a local running club that met every Sunday morning by the riverside.
all.zh: 我加入了一個當地跑步社團，每個星期天早上在河邊集合。

all.en: The members ranged from teenagers to retired runners with decades of experience.
all.zh: 成員從青少年到擁有數十年經驗的退休跑者都有。

all.en: They taught me how to pace myself by talking comfortably while running.
all.zh: 他們教我用能輕鬆聊天的方式來掌握配速。

all.en: During the summer, the morning heat became my biggest unexpected challenge.
all.zh: 夏天時，早晨的高溫成了我最意想不到的挑戰。

all.en: I learned to drink electrolyte water and to start runs before sunrise.
all.zh: 我學會喝電解質水，並在日出前開始跑步。

all.en: By autumn, I felt much stronger and ready to think about that full marathon.
all.zh: 到了秋天，我感覺強壯許多，已經準備好考慮那場全馬了。

all.en: In October, I signed up for my very first full marathon next spring.
all.zh: 十月時，我報名了明年春天的人生第一場全程馬拉松。

all.en: My coach designed a longer twenty-week plan with more cross-training and recovery days.
all.zh: 教練幫我安排了一份為期二十週的計畫，加入更多交叉訓練和恢復日。

all.en: I added regular cycling and pool workouts to protect my knees from too much pounding.
all.zh: 我加入了規律的騎車和游泳訓練，避免膝蓋承受過多衝擊。

all.en: My diet shifted toward more complex carbs, lean proteins, and plenty of leafy greens.
all.zh: 我的飲食轉向更多複合碳水、瘦肉蛋白和大量的綠色蔬菜。

all.en: On rainy mornings, I trained indoors on the treadmill while watching old running documentaries.
all.zh: 下雨的早晨，我會在跑步機上訓練，邊看以前的跑步紀錄片。

all.en: My running club organized monthly group runs through scenic trails outside the city.
all.zh: 我所屬的跑步社每個月都會在郊外風景優美的步道一起跑。

all.en: During one twenty-five-kilometer training run, I hit a difficult emotional wall around the eighteenth mark.
all.zh: 在一次二十五公里的訓練中，我在十八公里左右遇到了一道艱難的心理牆。

all.en: My teammates ran beside me and helped me push through with cheerful conversation.
all.zh: 隊友陪在我身旁，用愉快的聊天幫我撐了過去。

all.en: After that day, I trusted my body more than the small doubts in my head.
all.zh: 那天之後，我開始更相信身體，而不是腦中那些細小的懷疑。

all.en: Race day still feels distant, but every sunrise run brings me one step closer.
all.zh: 比賽日感覺還很遙遠，但每一次的日出晨跑都讓我更靠近終點線一步。

all.en: As the full marathon approached, my coach scheduled a final twenty-eight-kilometer practice run.
all.zh: 全程馬拉松逼近時，教練安排了一次最後的二十八公里練習跑。

all.en: I woke before sunrise, ate oatmeal with banana, and laced up my newest running shoes.
all.zh: 我在日出前起床，吃了香蕉燕麥粥，繫上我最新的一雙跑鞋。

all.en: My running club joined me along the river trail to provide steady company and support.
all.zh: 我的跑步社友沿著河岸步道陪我跑，提供穩定的陪伴與支持。

all.en: Around the twentieth kilometer, my left calf began to cramp painfully and slowed my pace.
all.zh: 大約跑到第二十公里時，我的左小腿開始抽筋疼痛，配速也慢了下來。

all.en: A teammate handed me electrolyte gel and reminded me to relax my breathing rhythm.
all.zh: 一位隊友遞給我電解質凝膠，提醒我放鬆呼吸的節奏。

all.en: I walked for two minutes and then carefully eased back into a gentle but steady jog.
all.zh: 我走了兩分鐘，然後小心地重新進入溫和但穩定的慢跑。

all.en: By the final stretch, my body felt tired but proud of how much it could handle.
all.zh: 到最後一段時，我的身體雖然疲憊，卻為自己能承受這麼多而驕傲。

all.en: My coach met us at the finish point with cold towels and warm congratulations for everyone.
all.zh: 教練在終點以冷毛巾和溫暖的祝賀迎接我們每一個人。

all.en: That night, I lay in bed and quietly trusted that race day would be possible.
all.zh: 那晚，我躺在床上，默默相信比賽日是可以完成的。

all.en: Training, I realized, was less about speed and more about meeting myself with patient courage.
all.zh: 我體會到，訓練重點並不在於速度，而在於用耐心和勇氣面對自己。

all.en: On the morning of the full marathon, light rain greeted thousands of nervous runners at the start.
all.zh: 全程馬拉松當天早晨，細雨迎接著起跑線上數千位緊張的跑者。

all.en: I tied my favorite green ribbon around my wrist as a small reminder of my coach.
all.zh: 我把最喜歡的綠色緞帶綁在手腕上，作為對教練的小小提醒。

all.en: My running club spread out along the route to cheer at the toughest uphill sections.
all.zh: 我的跑步社友分布在路線各處，在最陡峭的上坡段為大家加油。

all.en: At kilometer thirty, my legs felt heavy, but a familiar tune on my playlist lifted my mood.
all.zh: 跑到第三十公里時，雙腿沉重，但播放清單裡熟悉的旋律提振了我的心情。

all.en: Around kilometer thirty-five, a stranger handed me a slice of orange that tasted like sunshine.
all.zh: 在第三十五公里附近，一位陌生人遞給我一片柳橙，味道像陽光一樣甜美。

all.en: My coach waited at kilometer forty with a small towel and steady, encouraging eye contact.
all.zh: 教練在第四十公里處等我，手裡拿著小毛巾，眼神穩定地給我鼓勵。

all.en: Crossing the finish line, I cried quietly, hugging strangers who had run beside me for hours.
all.zh: 通過終點線時，我靜靜流淚，擁抱那些陪我跑了好幾小時的陌生人。

all.en: That night, I lay in a warm bath and thanked every patient kilometer for the lesson.
all.zh: 那晚，我泡在溫熱的浴缸裡，感謝每一公里耐心地教會我的功課。

all.en: My parents framed my finisher medal beside an old photo from my very first short run.
all.zh: 父母把我的完賽獎牌裱起來，掛在我第一次短跑的舊照片旁邊。

all.en: Distance, I finally understood, was simply patience wearing the shoes of careful daily practice.
all.zh: 我終於明白，距離不過就是穿著日常細心練習鞋的耐心。

all.en: Six months later, I quietly trained for my first full marathon, running gentle long routes every weekend.
all.zh: 六個月後，我默默為第一場全程馬拉松訓練，每個週末跑著溫柔的長距離路線。

all.en: My parents joined me at the starting line and held a small handwritten sign cheering my patient effort.
all.zh: 我父母在起跑線旁陪我，手裡舉著一塊為我耐心努力加油的小手寫看板。

all.en: I finished the marathon slowly but proudly, crossing the line as twilight gently approached the streets.
all.zh: 我緩慢卻自豪地完成馬拉松，在暮色溫柔降臨街道時越過終點線。

all.en: That evening, I dedicated my finisher medal to every single kilometer that had once felt completely impossible.
all.zh: 那晚，我把完賽獎牌獻給每一段曾經感覺完全不可能的公里。

all.en: A local newspaper interviewed me about training gently while balancing a busy nine-to-five office career.
all.zh: 一家當地報紙訪問我，談論在繁忙朝九晚五辦公室工作中如何溫柔訓練。

all.en: Several readers contacted me, asking for advice on starting their own gentle first running practice.
all.zh: 好幾位讀者聯絡我，徵詢開始自己第一次溫柔跑步練習的建議。

all.en: I began leading a small Saturday morning beginners group inside my neighborhood gentle community park.
all.zh: 我開始在社區溫柔的公園內帶領一個小型的週六晨間初學者跑團。

all.en: Older runners and shy teenagers stretched together every weekend before our slow patient practice loops.
all.zh: 年長跑者與害羞的青少年每個週末在我們緩慢耐心的練習圈前一起伸展。

all.en: My parents quietly attended every single meetup, distributing homemade rice balls and small bottles of water.
all.zh: 我父母默默出席每一次聚會，發放手作飯糰和小瓶裝水。

all.en: One first half-marathon had truly grown into a gentle weekend community of patient runners and tender encouragement.
all.zh: 一場第一次的半程馬拉松，真正成長為一個由耐心跑者與溫柔鼓勵組成的週末社群。

all.en: By the second year, our small beginner group grew into a beloved running family of forty patient members.
all.zh: 到了第二年，我們的小型初學者跑團成長為一個由四十位耐心成員組成的摯愛跑步家庭。

all.en: We organized a friendly local fun run with colorful headbands and homemade finisher ribbons for every participant.
all.zh: 我們籌辦了一場親切的當地趣味路跑，為每位參賽者準備繽紛頭帶與手作完賽緞帶。

all.en: My parents handed out the ribbons at the finish line, calling each runner by their warm first name.
all.zh: 我父母在終點線發送緞帶，溫暖地一一叫出每位跑者的名字。

all.en: A retired teacher in our group ran her very first three-kilometer race with tears of quiet pride.
all.zh: 我們團裡一位退休老師完成了她人生第一場三公里賽，眼中流下安靜驕傲的淚水。

all.en: Two shy teenagers became close friends and later joined the high school cross-country team together.
all.zh: 兩位害羞的青少年成了好朋友，後來一起加入了高中越野隊。

all.en: Our weekend route map was painted on a small wooden sign hung quietly near the community park entrance.
all.zh: 我們的週末路線圖被畫在一塊小木牌上，靜靜地掛在社區公園入口附近。

all.en: My old coach visited one Saturday and ran the full beginner loop with us at a gentle steady pace.
all.zh: 我以前的教練在某個週六造訪，以溫和穩定的配速與我們一起跑完整圈初學者路線。

all.en: He smiled afterward and said the group's patience reminded him of why he had begun coaching long ago.
all.zh: 跑完後他微笑著說，這個團體的耐心讓他想起當年自己開始擔任教練的初衷。

all.en: We celebrated the second anniversary with a quiet picnic, sharing fruit and stories under a wide oak tree.
all.zh: 我們以一場寧靜的野餐慶祝兩週年，在一棵寬大的橡樹下分享水果與故事。

all.en: One half-marathon goal had quietly stitched together neighbors, generations, and gentle Saturday sunrises forever.
all.zh: 一個半馬目標，悄悄地將鄰居、世代與溫柔的週六日出永遠縫合在一起。

all.en: Our city's parks department donated paint to refresh the wooden route sign with brighter morning colors.
all.zh: 本市公園處捐贈油漆，為那塊木製路線指示牌補上更明亮的晨間色彩。

all.en: Two members of our group volunteered as official course marshals during the city's annual charity run.
all.zh: 我們團裡兩位成員自願擔任本市年度慈善路跑的官方賽道工作人員。

all.en: We added a quiet stretching circle before each Saturday run, led by a gentle retired physical therapist.
all.zh: 我們在每個週六晨跑前增設了一個寧靜的伸展圈，由一位溫柔的退休物理治療師帶領。

all.en: On rainy mornings, members brought hot oatmeal in thermoses to share beneath the wide park pavilion.
all.zh: 下雨的早晨，成員們會帶來保溫瓶裝的熱燕麥粥，在寬廣的公園涼亭下共享。

all.en: A young father started bringing his preschool son in a small jogging stroller every other gentle weekend.
all.zh: 一位年輕父親開始每隔一個溫柔的週末，用慢跑推車帶著他學齡前的兒子一起來。

all.en: His son's giggles became a treasured morning soundtrack for the entire patient community of grateful runners.
all.zh: 他兒子的咯咯笑聲，成為整個耐心而感恩的跑者社群最珍貴的晨間配樂。

all.en: We collected each member's favorite quiet running mantra and printed them onto small laminated cards.
all.zh: 我們收集每位成員最愛的安靜跑步座右銘，印製成小型護貝卡片。

all.en: New runners received a card on their first morning, tucked gently into their welcome packet envelopes.
all.zh: 新跑者在第一次出席時會收到一張卡，輕輕夾在他們的歡迎信封裡。

all.en: By the third anniversary, three other neighborhoods had launched their own gentle Saturday beginner running circles.
all.zh: 到第三週年時，已有另外三個社區成立了他們自己溫柔的週六初學者跑團。

all.en: We met annually at a quiet riverside park, sharing rice balls, slow runs, and one big patient family photo.
all.zh: 我們每年在一個安靜的河濱公園相聚，分享飯糰、慢跑，並拍下一張溫柔耐心的大家庭合照。

all.en: Last Saturday morning, it poured rain just as I was about to head out for my long run.
all.zh: 上週六早上，我正要出門跑長距離時，雨就嘩啦啦地下了起來。

all.en: I almost went back to bed, but my dad sent me a photo of his coffee and one waving hand.
all.zh: 我差點回床上躺平，但爸爸傳來一張他咖啡的照片和一個揮手的圖案。

all.en: I put on my rain jacket and met him by the park gate ten minutes later.
all.zh: 我穿上雨衣，十分鐘後在公園門口跟他會合。

all.en: He had brought a thermos of warm ginger tea wrapped inside a small old towel.
all.zh: 他帶來一壺溫熱的薑茶，外面用一條舊毛巾包著。

all.en: We ran slowly through empty trails, and the rain made every leaf look softly polished.
all.zh: 我們在空無一人的步道上慢慢跑著，雨水讓每片葉子都像被柔柔擦亮。

all.en: Halfway through, I slipped on a wet wooden bridge but caught myself on the handrail.
all.zh: 跑到一半，我在溼滑的木橋上滑了一下，幸好抓住了扶手。

all.en: My dad laughed and said even seasoned runners learn to respect every single rainy mile.
all.zh: 爸爸笑著說，連資深跑者也得學會敬重每一公里的雨中路。

all.en: We finished only ten kilometers that day, but we sat afterward on a covered bench and chatted.
all.zh: 那天我們只跑了十公里，但跑完後我們坐在有遮雨棚的長椅上聊了好久。

all.en: He told me stories about his own first race when he was nineteen and full of pride.
all.zh: 他跟我講起他十九歲時第一場比賽的故事，那時他滿心驕傲。

all.en: I realized I had never asked him about his running youth, and I felt grateful for the rain.
all.zh: 我才發現自己從沒問過他年輕時跑步的事，反而感激這場雨。

all.en: On the walk home, we promised to make rainy Saturday runs a small private father-daughter tradition.
all.zh: 走回家的路上，我們約好把雨天的週六晨跑變成我們父女之間一個小小的私房傳統。
